description: inspect on a minimal notebook prints public JSON with format, paired paths, version, and cells.
Missing notebook cell ids are normalized to c1, c2 in cell order.
Duplicate cell ids in .ipynb input fail before writing output files.
inspect parses percent header minijupy and kernelspec metadata.
Percent markers parse code, markdown, raw, id, tags, and name fields.
to-text writes percent header and markers with ids while omitting ipynb execution outputs.
Config base directory is the config file parent when deriving mapped paired paths.
Default same-directory pairing swaps .ipynb and .py extensions.
Directory mapping derives notebook counterpart from a script under script_dir.
to-text converts an ipynb to percent and strips execution_count and outputs from text.
to-ipynb converts percent text into a normalized notebook with null execution counts and empty outputs.
pair accepts .ipynb input, sets pairing metadata, and creates the percent counterpart.
pair rejects percent-script input and writes no notebook counterpart.
sync uses the representation with the greater version as source.
sync --source overrides version comparison.
When text is authoritative, sync preserves existing ipynb outputs and execution_count for matching cell ids.
inspect drops unsupported percent metadata fields from normalized cells without failing.
status reports paired paths, source, would_write, roundtrip status, missing paths, and errors without writing files.
Malformed percent marker JSON fails and creates no output notebook.
Directory-mapping config fails when input is outside the configured side and writes nothing.
ipynb -> percent -> ipynb preserves representable model fields across both conversions.
Newer text drives sync, updates the notebook, preserves outputs by id, and status then reports clean state.
Newer ipynb drives sync, updates percent text, and status agrees afterward.
Notebook metadata, cell ids, tags, and names fan out through percent text, rebuilt ipynb, inspect, and status.
Config directory mapping drives pair, sync, and status across notebook and script directories.
status.would_write predicts sync writes, then status becomes clean after sync.
Authoritative text can change sources and cell order while matching ids preserve ipynb outputs for surviving cells.
Equal versions with no --source make sync a no-op and status source none with no writes.
--source override controls files and subsequent status even when the other side has a higher version.
status reports a missing counterpart; pair creates it; sync then no-ops consistently.
Cells without ids receive public c1/c2 ids and positional output fallback preserves existing outputs during sync.
Unsupported metadata is dropped consistently across inspect, text conversion, and rebuilt notebook outputs.
Malformed authoritative ipynb fails sync and leaves both paired files unchanged.
A directory mapping mismatch during sync fails and leaves existing files unchanged.
